title: dodecatheon pauciflorum subsp. eupauciflorum
intro: Dodecatheon pauciflorum subsp. eupauciflorum, also known as the Nootka shooting star or the Nootka dodecatheon, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its bell-shaped flowers and distinctive growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dodecatheon pauciflorum subsp. eupauciflorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made through infusion or decoction, and it is often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
